data.title: Investors of Skye Bioscience targeted for investigation by Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP. Losses between Nov 4, 2024, and Oct 3, 2025 to be discussed.
data.description: Securities Litigation Partner James (Josh) Wilson from Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP is urging investors who incurred losses in Skye to reach out to him directly for a consultation regarding their potential options. If you bought or obtained securities in Skye within the period from November 4, 2024, to October 3, 2025, and wish to explore this further, please contact Mr. Wilson.

data.title: Northfield and Juno are pleased with a new deal to speed up Ring of Fire development.
data.description: The latest report indicates that the global economy is expected to grow by 4.3% in 2022, an increase from the 3.9% growth forecasted earlier. This improved projection is fueled by strong performances in the United States, with a projected growth rate of 4.7%, and China, with a growth rate of 5.9%. The Eurozone is also expected to see growth of 4.3%. However, concerns linger over inflation rates, with global inflation predicted to hit 3.7% in 2022, up from the previous 3.2% estimate. This news suggests a promising outlook for the global economy in the coming year despite inflation challenges.

data.title: Andrew Grenville, ex-CEO of Inpria, joins Phosio's Board to boost AR/AI glasses launch. Brings expertise in materials from sale of Inpria to JSR Corporation.
data.description: A company based in Corvallis, Oregon called Phosio has recently welcomed Andrew Grenville, the former CEO of Inpria, to its Board of Directors. Grenville is renowned in the field of advanced materials and semiconductor innovation. He has a wealth of experience in overseeing the development of materials platforms from their initial stages to large-scale manufacturing and strategic acquisition. Notably, Grenville played a key role in the acquisition of Inpria by JSR Corporation. Omid Sadeghi, a representative of Phosio, expressed excitement about Grenville joining the board.

data.title: Behavox reports 30%+ 2025 growth in AI-powered surveillance and releases upgrades, marking successful year.
data.description: A company based in London and Montreal, Behavox, has unveiled the latest enhancements to Behavox Quantum, its primary compliance platform. The upgrades come after a successful year that saw a substantial growth of over 30% in Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) for the year 2025. This growth was primarily fueled by the increasing need for AI-driven surveillance tools that offer transparency, control, and quantifiable results. Compliance teams worldwide rely on Behavox Quantum to detect potential issues.

data.title: MinIO launches AIStor Free and Enterprise Lite tiers to expand accessibility for AI and analytics uses.
data.description: MinIO, headquartered in Redwood City, California, has introduced AIStor Free and AIStor Enterprise Lite to expand the accessibility of MinIO AIStor for various AI and analytics applications. These new offerings aim to cater to a broader range of use cases, providing enhanced options for users.

data.title: Teachers Federal Credit Union donates $50,000 during its 2025 Season of Giving to support various charities.
data.description: The Season of Giving in 2025 is backing Johns Hopkins All Children's Hospital, Long Island Cares, Inc., and Paws of War in Hauppauge, New York. Teachers Federal Credit Union, a major credit union in the U.S. holding over $9.9 billion in assets, is spearheading this initiative, aiming to make a positive impact in the community.

data.title: Wide Bandgap Semiconductor Market to reach USD 4.66 Billion by 2032 at 12.6% CAGR, driven by demand in EVs and 5G.
data.description: The Wide Bandgap Semiconductor Market is experiencing significant growth as the demand for energy-efficient power devices surges across various sectors. This includes electric vehicles, renewable energy systems, fast chargers, data centers, and 5G infrastructure. Key materials driving this growth are silicon carbide (SiC) and gallium nitride. The market is currently valued at approximately $X billion and is expected to reach around $Y billion by 20ZZ. This growth is propelled by the increasing adoption of advanced technologies for power electronics and the shift towards sustainable energy solutions.
